name: Barbell Back Squat
evidence_rationale: Foundational bilateral compound for lower-body strength and hypertrophy; high evidence base for quad/glute development.

name: Front Squat
evidence_rationale: More quad-dominant and torso-upright than the back squat; reduces lower-back shear load while still building bilateral leg strength.

name: Goblet Squat
evidence_rationale: Easier bar-path and depth cueing than a barbell squat; good entry point for squat-pattern volume without heavy axial load.

name: Smith Machine Squat
evidence_rationale: Fixed bar path lets lifters push closer to failure safely; useful for hypertrophy accumulation without a spotter.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
evidence_rationale: Single-leg squat pattern that corrects left/right strength asymmetry and trains hip stability through a long range of motion.

name: Cossack Squat
evidence_rationale: Lateral squat variant building ankle and hip mobility under load through a wide deep stance; complements ATG split squat's sagittal-plane ankle work with a frontal-plane challenge.

name: Leg Press
evidence_rationale: Machine-stabilized bilateral squat pattern; lets lifters load volume safely without balance/bracing demands.

name: Hack Squat
evidence_rationale: Fixed-path machine squat emphasizing quads with reduced lower-back shear versus a free barbell squat.

name: Pistol Squat
evidence_rationale: Advanced unilateral bodyweight squat building single-leg strength, ankle dorsiflexion, and balance simultaneously.

name: Box Squat
evidence_rationale: Teaches hip-hinge depth control and posterior-chain engagement out of the bottom of a squat; common powerlifting accessory.

name: Walking Lunge
evidence_rationale: Dynamic unilateral lower-body pattern that builds single-leg strength and balance through a long stride.

name: Step-Up
evidence_rationale: Unilateral squat-pattern movement that closely mirrors stair/uneven-terrain demand; strong carryover to daily function.

name: Wall Sit
evidence_rationale: Isometric squat hold; low-impact way to build quad endurance without joint loading, useful on lower-readiness days.

name: Box Jump
evidence_rationale: Explosive triple-extension power development; CLAUDE.md flags plyometrics as part of the program's high-leverage toolkit.

name: Jump Squat
evidence_rationale: Loads the squat pattern with explosive intent; builds rate of force development on top of squat strength.

name: Asian Squat Hold
evidence_rationale: Sustained flat-footed deep-squat hold variant emphasizing ankle dorsiflexion specifically; a common assessment and training tool for ankle/hip mobility ceiling.

name: Single-Leg Balance (eyes closed)
evidence_rationale: Proprioceptive ankle/balance work; tracks single-leg stability improvement directly relevant to lateral-movement injury risk.

name: Single-Leg Balance on Pillow
evidence_rationale: Unstable-surface balance progression beyond the eyes-closed floor variant; builds ankle proprioception under perturbation.

name: Bosu Ball Squat
evidence_rationale: Combines squat-pattern strength with unstable-surface balance demand; bridges strength and stability training.

name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift Balance Reach
evidence_rationale: Unilateral balance-under-load challenge that doubles as posterior-chain activation; strong carryover to pickleball's single-leg landings.

name: Weighted Ankle Dorsiflexion Mobilization
evidence_rationale: Loaded knee-to-wall mobilization using a light external load at the knee; progresses the unloaded wall ankle test once baseline dorsiflexion range improves.

name: Half-Kneeling Ankle Mobilization
evidence_rationale: Half-kneeling rock-forward drill loading dorsiflexion under bodyweight; a no-equipment complement to the banded ankle distraction for days without a band.

name: Ankle Circles
evidence_rationale: Simple active range-of-motion warmup; loosens the ankle joint capsule before loading.

name: Calf Raise Stretch on Step
evidence_rationale: Static calf/Achilles stretch off a step edge; addresses posterior ankle tightness that limits dorsiflexion.

name: Seated Ankle Dorsiflexion with Band
evidence_rationale: Banded mobilization targeting dorsiflexion specifically; complements the standing banded distraction with a seated, lower-load variant.

name: Single-Leg Calf Raise
evidence_rationale: Builds calf strength and ankle-stabilizer endurance unilaterally -- directly protective against the lateral-pickleball reinjury pattern CLAUDE.md flags.

name: Glute-Ham Raise
evidence_rationale: Machine-assisted hamstring-curl movement sharing the Nordic curl's eccentric-loading evidence base; a regression option when bodyweight Nordic curls are too advanced.

name: Active Straight-Leg Raise Hamstring Stretch
evidence_rationale: Supine active-assisted hamstring stretch using a strap; complements the PNF contract-relax method with an active end-range hold.

name: Deep Lunge Hip Opener
evidence_rationale: Kneeling deep-lunge hip-opener stretch; complements the 90/90's rotational hip work with an extension-biased anterior-hip target.

name: Half-Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch with Reach
evidence_rationale: Active variant of the couch stretch adding an overhead reach to bias the rectus femoris and add a thoracic-extension component.

name: Standing Hamstring Stretch
evidence_rationale: Simple static hamstring stretch; low-skill complement to the PNF variant on lower-readiness days.

name: Pigeon Pose
evidence_rationale: Deep hip external-rotator stretch; common yoga pose with strong carryover to hip-opening goals CLAUDE.md emphasizes.

name: Frog Stretch
evidence_rationale: Adductor/hip-opener stretch complementing the 90/90 and couch stretch from a different angle of hip mobility.

name: World's Greatest Stretch
evidence_rationale: Combination lunge-rotation-hamstring stretch; efficient multi-joint mobility movement for a pre-lift warmup or recovery day.

name: Side-Lying Hip Adduction
evidence_rationale: Side-lying adductor-strengthening movement; a lower-skill regression toward the full Copenhagen plank for building the same adductor/groin strength.

name: Adductor Stretch (seated)
evidence_rationale: Static adductor/groin stretch; complements the Copenhagen plank's strength work with passive range-of-motion.

name: Romanian Deadlift
evidence_rationale: Hip-hinge hypertrophy/strength staple targeting the posterior chain through a long hamstring range of motion.

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
evidence_rationale: Lower-load equipment variant of the barbell RDL; more accessible bar path for hip-hinge skill development.

name: Conventional Deadlift
evidence_rationale: Full-body hip-hinge strength staple; among the highest-loading compound exercises for total posterior-chain development.

name: Trap Bar Deadlift
evidence_rationale: More upright torso position than a conventional deadlift, reducing lower-back shear while keeping the hip-hinge loading benefit.

name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
evidence_rationale: Unilateral hip-hinge that trains hamstring strength and balance together; closes left/right asymmetry per CLAUDE.md's unilateral-from-the-left principle.

name: Cable Pull-Through
evidence_rationale: Reinforces hip-hinge mechanics under constant cable tension; lower skill demand than a barbell hinge for the same posterior-chain target.

name: Glute Bridge
evidence_rationale: Foundational glute-activation hinge pattern; useful as both a warmup primer and a standalone hypertrophy movement.

name: Hip Thrust
evidence_rationale: Loaded glute-focused hinge variant with strong evidence for glute hypertrophy specifically versus squats/deadlifts alone.

name: Good Morning
evidence_rationale: Hip-hinge accessory that isolates posterior-chain strength under bar load without the grip/setup demand of a deadlift.

name: Kettlebell Swing
evidence_rationale: Ballistic hip-hinge that builds explosive posterior-chain power and conditioning simultaneously.

name: Standing Toe Touch with Reach-Through
evidence_rationale: Standing segmental spinal-flexion stretch reaching toward the floor; a bodyweight-only complement to the loaded Jefferson curl for days without a barbell.

name: Bird Dog
evidence_rationale: Anti-rotation core-stability movement with strong evidence for safe lower-back loading and motor control.

name: Plank
evidence_rationale: Foundational anti-extension core hold; baseline core-endurance movement with minimal equipment demand.

name: Side Plank
evidence_rationale: Anti-lateral-flexion core hold; trains the obliques and quadratus lumborum in a way a front plank does not.

name: Hanging Leg Raise
evidence_rationale: Loaded anti-extension/flexion core movement with strong hypertrophy carryover to visible lower-ab definition.

name: Cable Woodchop
evidence_rationale: Rotational core-strength movement; trains the anti-rotation/rotation pattern pickleball's swing mechanics demand.

name: Dead Bug
evidence_rationale: Low-load anti-extension core movement with excellent motor-control carryover; common physical-therapy staple for lower-back resilience.

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
evidence_rationale: Advanced anti-extension core movement under long lever arm; high core-strength carryover once plank/dead-bug progressions are mastered.

name: Hollow Body Hold
evidence_rationale: Gymnastics-derived core-bracing hold; builds the full-body tension skill that transfers to bracing under a barbell.

name: Cat-Cow
evidence_rationale: Dynamic spinal-segment mobility flow; gentle warmup movement for the whole spine before loaded work.

name: Wall-Supported Chin Tuck
evidence_rationale: Wall-supported variant of the chin tuck adding tactile feedback for correct cervical retraction depth; useful cueing progression for forward-head posture correction.

name: Thoracic Rotation on Foam Roller
evidence_rationale: Foam-roller-supported thoracic rotation drill; complements the extension-only foam-roller variant with a rotational axis of thoracic mobility.

name: Scalene Stretch
evidence_rationale: Targets the scalene muscle group from a different angle than the levator scapulae stretch; both commonly co-tight contributors to chronic neck stiffness.

name: Neck Rotation Stretch
evidence_rationale: Active rotational neck range-of-motion movement; complements the levator scapulae and chin-tuck work from a different plane of motion.

name: Upper Trap Stretch
evidence_rationale: Lateral-flexion stretch targeting the upper trapezius, a common source of referred neck tension alongside the levator scapulae.

name: Scapular Wall Slides
evidence_rationale: Reinforces scapular upward rotation and thoracic extension together; corrective groundwork before overhead pressing.

name: Thread the Needle
evidence_rationale: Rotational thoracic-spine mobility movement performed on all fours; complements the foam-roller extension with a rotational axis.

name: Standing Forward Fold with Shoulder Flexion
evidence_rationale: Standing variant combining a hamstring stretch with overhead shoulder flexion; a no-mat alternative to the down dog progression with a similar combined-mobility target.

name: Cross-Arm Internal Rotation Stretch (right)
evidence_rationale: Standing internal-rotation stretch targeting the same posterior shoulder-capsule tightness as the sleeper stretch from a standing position rather than side-lying.

name: Wall Slide with External Rotation
evidence_rationale: Combines the scapular wall slide with an external-rotation finish; corrective groundwork for shoulder-capsule health distinct from the v1 Shoulder CARs entry.

name: Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
evidence_rationale: Simple posterior-deltoid stretch; lower-skill complement to the sleeper stretch for general shoulder tightness.

name: Doorway Pec Stretch
evidence_rationale: Anterior-chain shoulder/chest stretch; counters the internally-rotated posture that exacerbates right-shoulder tightness.

name: Band Pull-Apart
evidence_rationale: Light high-rep posterior-shoulder/upper-back activation movement; common prehab staple for shoulder-health and posture.

name: Face Pull
evidence_rationale: Targets the rear delts and external rotators directly; widely used corrective accessory for shoulder-health and posture balance.

name: Barbell Bench Press
evidence_rationale: Foundational horizontal-press compound; among the most evidence-backed movements for chest/shoulder/triceps strength and size.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
evidence_rationale: Greater range of motion and unilateral stabilizer demand than the barbell variant, with lower injury risk from a fixed bar path.

name: Incline Barbell Press
evidence_rationale: Upper-chest-emphasis press variant; fills out the shoulder girdle in a way flat pressing alone does not.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
evidence_rationale: Equipment variant of the incline press with a longer range of motion and independent-arm stabilizer demand.

name: Incline Smith Machine Press
evidence_rationale: Fixed-path incline press variant; lets a lifter push closer to failure safely without a spotter.

name: Overhead Press
evidence_rationale: Vertical-press compound building shoulder strength and stability through full overhead range -- directly relevant to filling out the shoulder girdle.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
evidence_rationale: Dumbbell variant of the overhead press; independent-arm path helps correct left/right shoulder imbalance.

name: Push-Up
evidence_rationale: Bodyweight horizontal-press staple; scalable, equipment-free option for chest/shoulder/triceps strength.

name: Dip
evidence_rationale: Bodyweight vertical-press movement emphasizing triceps and lower chest under a deep stretch range.

name: Cable Chest Fly
evidence_rationale: Isolation movement for chest under constant cable tension; complements pressing with a stretch-focused hypertrophy stimulus.

name: Landmine Press
evidence_rationale: Shoulder-friendly pressing angle that reduces impingement risk while still building pressing strength; good option on a tender-shoulder day.

name: Plyo Push-Up
evidence_rationale: Explosive upper-body pressing power movement; builds rate of force development for sports requiring fast hand/arm action.

name: Medicine Ball Chest Pass
evidence_rationale: Explosive horizontal-press power movement transferable to pickleball's quick reactive shots.

name: Barbell Row
evidence_rationale: Foundational horizontal-pull compound; builds upper-back thickness directly addressing CLAUDE.md's "fill out shoulders/upper back" goal.

name: Dumbbell Row
evidence_rationale: Unilateral horizontal-pull variant; trains each side of the upper back independently, supporting left/right correction.

name: Cable Row
evidence_rationale: Constant-tension horizontal-pull movement; easier on the lower back than a bent-over barbell row for high-volume back work.

name: Pull-Up
evidence_rationale: Vertical-pull bodyweight staple with strong evidence for lat/upper-back strength and width.

name: Chin-Up
evidence_rationale: Supinated-grip pull-up variant with greater biceps involvement; useful pulling-strength progression alongside the pull-up.

name: Lat Pulldown
evidence_rationale: Machine vertical-pull movement; accessible regression toward a full pull-up while still building lat width.

name: Single-Arm Lat Pulldown
evidence_rationale: Unilateral vertical-pull variant; trains each lat independently, directly supporting CLAUDE.md's left-first unilateral-correction principle.

name: Face-Down Reverse Fly
evidence_rationale: Rear-delt isolation movement on a bench; reinforces the posterior shoulder strength the face pull also targets.

name: Seated Cable Row
evidence_rationale: Seated horizontal-pull variant with strict torso bracing; good high-volume upper-back accessory.

name: Inverted Row
evidence_rationale: Bodyweight horizontal-pull regression using a bar or rings; useful entry point toward weighted rowing strength.

name: Barbell Curl
evidence_rationale: Classic biceps isolation movement; supports visible arm definition alongside the program's compound-first emphasis.

name: Dumbbell Curl
evidence_rationale: Unilateral biceps isolation; independent-arm path supports correcting right-arm dominance.

name: Hammer Curl
evidence_rationale: Neutral-grip biceps/forearm variant; adds forearm and brachialis development alongside standard curls.

name: Tricep Pushdown
evidence_rationale: Isolation movement for triceps under constant cable tension; complements compound pressing for arm definition.

name: Overhead Tricep Extension
evidence_rationale: Stretch-focused triceps isolation movement; targets the long head more than pushdown variants.

name: Wrist Curl
evidence_rationale: Direct forearm-flexor strengthening; protective for wrist-loaded lifts like front squats and overhead pressing.

name: Reverse Wrist Curl
evidence_rationale: Forearm-extensor strengthening that balances the wrist curl's flexor work; protective against wrist overuse.

name: Wrist Mobility Flow (flexion/extension/circles)
evidence_rationale: Combined active-mobility flow for the wrist joint; useful pre-lift prep before any bar-loaded pressing or front-rack work.

name: Farmer's Carry
evidence_rationale: Loaded carry building grip, forearm, and trunk stability simultaneously; high carryover to real-world load-bearing tasks.

name: Suitcase Carry
evidence_rationale: Unilateral loaded carry that challenges anti-lateral-flexion core stability alongside grip strength.

name: Lateral Lunge
evidence_rationale: Frontal-plane lunge variant; directly trains the lateral movement pattern pickleball demands, beyond sagittal-plane squats/lunges.

name: Lateral Band Walk
evidence_rationale: Glute-medius activation movement under lateral resistance; a common pickleball/lateral-sport injury-prevention staple.

name: Monster Walk
evidence_rationale: Multi-directional banded walking drill that builds hip-abductor and ankle-stabilizer endurance together.

name: Lateral Hop
evidence_rationale: Single-plane plyometric hop building lateral-landing control directly relevant to pickleball's side-to-side footwork and ankle reinjury risk.

name: Skater Hop
evidence_rationale: Lateral bounding plyometric that mirrors pickleball's lateral cutting demand while training single-leg landing absorption.

name: Depth Jump
evidence_rationale: Reactive-strength plyometric using a drop into an immediate jump; builds fast eccentric-to-concentric force transfer.

name: Broad Jump
evidence_rationale: Horizontal explosive triple-extension movement; complements the vertical box jump with a different force vector.

name: Tuck Jump
evidence_rationale: High-intensity plyometric demanding rapid hip flexion under flight time; advanced progression once landing mechanics are solid.

name: Med Ball Slam
evidence_rationale: Explosive total-body power movement through the core and shoulders; doubles as a high-intensity conditioning finisher.

name: Med Ball Rotational Throw
evidence_rationale: Explosive rotational power movement mirroring the trunk-rotation demand of a pickleball groundstroke.

name: Plyo Lunge (alternating jump lunge)
evidence_rationale: Explosive alternating-leg lunge; builds single-leg power and reactive landing control together.

name: Pickleball-Specific Lateral Shuffle
evidence_rationale: Sport-specific lateral conditioning drill mirroring pickleball court movement; doubles as dynamic ankle/hip warmup before play.

name: Jump Rope
evidence_rationale: Low-equipment cardio/conditioning movement that also reinforces ankle stiffness and rhythm under repeated ground contact.

name: Treadmill Incline Walk
evidence_rationale: Low-impact steady-state cardio option; useful active-recovery alternative to a full run on a lower-readiness day.

name: Easy Aerobic Run
evidence_rationale: Base-building steady-state run; the foundation CLAUDE.md's 10%/week progression cap is built around.

name: Interval Sprints
evidence_rationale: Higher-intensity running variant for VO2/speed development once aerobic base and hamstring resilience (Nordic curls) are established.

name: Stationary Bike
evidence_rationale: Low-impact cardio alternative that maintains aerobic conditioning while fully offloading the ankles -- useful during an ankle flare-up.

name: Rowing Machine
evidence_rationale: Full-body, low-impact cardio modality that also reinforces hip-hinge and pulling-pattern endurance.

name: Battle Ropes
evidence_rationale: High-intensity upper-body conditioning movement; builds shoulder/grip endurance under a metabolic-conditioning stimulus.

name: Sled Push
evidence_rationale: Concentric-only loaded conditioning movement; builds leg drive and aerobic capacity with minimal eccentric/soreness cost.

name: Sled Drag (reverse)
evidence_rationale: Reverse sled drag is a common knee/ankle-friendly conditioning and rehab movement with very low joint shear.

name: Farmer's Carry Conditioning Circuit
evidence_rationale: Combines loaded-carry strength with a conditioning stimulus when performed for extended distance/time.

name: Mountain Climbers
evidence_rationale: Dynamic core-and-conditioning movement combining a plank hold with continuous hip flexion under tempo.

name: Burpee
evidence_rationale: High-intensity total-body conditioning movement combining a push-up, squat, and jump in one sequence.

name: Kettlebell Swing Conditioning Set
evidence_rationale: Extended-set kettlebell swing work shifts the same hip-hinge pattern from strength/power toward a conditioning stimulus.

name: Standing Quad Stretch
evidence_rationale: Simple static quad/knee-extensor stretch; useful complement to the couch stretch for anterior thigh tightness.

name: Foam Roll Quads
evidence_rationale: Self-myofascial release for the quads; common pre/post-session recovery tool to reduce stiffness.

name: Foam Roll IT Band
evidence_rationale: Self-myofascial release targeting the IT band, a common source of lateral knee/hip tightness in runners and lateral-sport athletes.

name: Calf Foam Roll
evidence_rationale: Self-myofascial release for the calves; complements the banded ankle distraction by addressing soft-tissue restriction.

name: Childs Pose
evidence_rationale: Restorative yoga pose lengthening the lats and lower back; common cool-down/recovery-day movement.

name: Knee-to-Chest Stretch
evidence_rationale: Simple supine lower-back/hip stretch; gentle option on a high-soreness or low-readiness day.

name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
evidence_rationale: Unilateral hinge variant isolating one side's glute/hamstring strength; supports left-first correction work.

name: Clamshell
evidence_rationale: Classic glute-medius activation/rehab movement; common corrective exercise for hip-stability deficits feeding into knee/ankle issues.

name: Fire Hydrant
evidence_rationale: Hip-abductor activation movement on all fours; complements the clamshell and lateral band walk for glute-medius strength.

name: Standing Hip Flexor Stretch
evidence_rationale: Standing variant of the couch stretch's anterior-hip target; useful when no bench/wall is available (e.g. between pickleball games).

name: 90/90 Hip Switch
evidence_rationale: Dynamic transition variant of the static 90/90 stretch; builds active internal/external hip rotation control, not just passive range.

name: Reverse Nordic Curl
evidence_rationale: Eccentric quad-strengthening movement using a kneeling lowering pattern; common knee-health corrective alongside the (hamstring) Nordic curl.

name: Terminal Knee Extension
evidence_rationale: Banded knee-extension corrective targeting the VMO; widely used in knee-rehab programming for joint stability.

name: Seated Figure-4 Stretch
evidence_rationale: Seated piriformis/glute stretch; lower-skill alternative to the pigeon pose for hip external-rotator tightness.

name: Banded Shoulder External Rotation
evidence_rationale: Rotator-cuff strengthening/corrective movement; protective against the impingement risk a tight right shoulder carries into pressing work.

name: Y-T-W Raises
evidence_rationale: Light-load scapular-stabilizer activation sequence; common shoulder-health prehab movement done before or after pressing.

name: Towel Behind-Back Shoulder Stretch
evidence_rationale: Internal/external rotation stretch using a towel; targets the same posterior-capsule tightness as the sleeper stretch from a different angle.

name: Standing Calf Raise
evidence_rationale: Direct calf-strengthening movement; complements the single-leg calf raise with a bilateral, higher-load variant.

name: Seated Calf Raise
evidence_rationale: Machine/seated calf variant emphasizing the soleus through a bent-knee position, complementing the standing gastrocnemius-biased raise.

name: Eccentric Heel Drop
evidence_rationale: Slow-eccentric calf-loading movement commonly used in Achilles-tendon rehab protocols; protective for ankle-adjacent tendon health.

name: Toe Yoga
evidence_rationale: Intrinsic-foot-muscle control drill; builds the small stabilizer strength that underlies ankle stability higher up the chain.

name: Foot Doming (short foot exercise)
evidence_rationale: Arch-activation drill building intrinsic foot strength; common foundation movement in ankle-rehab and balance programming.

name: Calf Raise on Step (eyes closed)
evidence_rationale: Combines calf strengthening with a balance challenge by removing visual feedback; progression beyond the basic single-leg balance drill.

name: Single-Leg Deadlift Reach to Cone
evidence_rationale: Functional balance drill combining hip-hinge mechanics with a reaching balance challenge; strong carryover to pickleball's lunging reach.

name: Bosu Ball Single-Leg Stand
evidence_rationale: Unstable-surface single-leg balance progression beyond a flat floor; builds ankle-stabilizer strength under perturbation.

name: Tandem Stance Hold
evidence_rationale: Heel-to-toe static balance hold; simple progression step between double-leg and single-leg balance work.

name: Y-Balance Reach
evidence_rationale: Multi-directional single-leg reaching balance drill widely used as both an assessment and a training tool for lower-limb stability.

name: Agility Ladder Drill
evidence_rationale: Fast-footwork conditioning drill that builds the rapid ankle-stabilization demand pickleball's quick direction changes require.

name: Cone Shuttle Run
evidence_rationale: Multi-directional sprint-and-cut conditioning drill mirroring real pickleball movement demand more closely than linear running alone.

name: Resistance Band Shoulder Pass-Through
evidence_rationale: Dynamic overhead-mobility drill using a band; good pre-press warmup that doubles as a shoulder-health mobility movement.

name: Arm Circles
evidence_rationale: Simple dynamic shoulder warmup movement; low-skill entry point before loaded overhead or pressing work.

name: Hip Circles
evidence_rationale: Dynamic hip-joint warmup movement; loosens the hip capsule through a full circular range before squatting or lunging.

name: Leg Swings
evidence_rationale: Dynamic swinging warmup for the hip and hamstring; common pre-run or pre-lift mobility primer.

name: Neck Isometric Holds (4-way)
evidence_rationale: Light isometric resistance in flexion/extension/lateral directions builds deep cervical stabilizer endurance, addressing chronic neck stiffness at its strength root rather than only its mobility side.

name: Prone Neck Retraction with Extension
evidence_rationale: Combines the chin-tuck retraction with thoracic extension lying face-down; reinforces the CLAUDE.md thoracic-spine-is-the-root-cause framing in one movement.

name: Suboccipital Release (massage ball)
evidence_rationale: Self-myofascial release at the skull base targeting the suboccipital muscles, a common contributor to chronic neck tension and tension headaches.

name: Cervical Lateral Flexion Stretch
evidence_rationale: Static side-bend neck stretch isolating the upper trap/scalene group from a different angle than the upper trap stretch's shoulder-anchored variant.

name: Plank with Shoulder Taps
evidence_rationale: Anti-rotation plank variant that adds an unstable-arm-lift balance challenge on top of the static core hold.

name: Cobra Stretch
evidence_rationale: Gentle prone spinal-extension stretch; common cool-down movement countering the spinal flexion of core/ab work.

name: Seated Wide-Leg Forward Fold
evidence_rationale: Seated bilateral hamstring/adductor stretch performed in a position similar to a row's seated base, complementing pulling-pattern training days.

name: Renegade Row
evidence_rationale: Unilateral rowing movement performed from a plank position under reactive core-stability demand; combines pulling strength with anti-rotation bracing power.

name: Single-Arm Band Row to Balance Reach
evidence_rationale: Combines a unilateral rowing pull with a single-leg balance hold, training upper-back pulling strength and ankle stability together.

name: Push-Up to Single-Arm Reach
evidence_rationale: Adds a single-arm balance/reach component at the top of a push-up, training pressing strength alongside shoulder and wrist stability.

name: Wrestler's Bridge Hold
evidence_rationale: Supported neck/upper-back bridge hold building neck-extensor endurance and balance together; performed lightly and supported, not a high-load wrestling bridge.
